typecheck.observation: [codex-review-guidelines] OK and [enterprise-policy] OK after strict review checks for the docs/governance packet

changedFilesCoverage.command: python3 scripts/changed_files_coverage.py --jacoco artifacts/pr-jacoco-local/jacoco-unit.xml --jacoco artifacts/pr-jacoco-local/jacoco-codered-access.xml --diff-base "$(git merge-base origin/main HEAD)" --src-root erp-domain/src/main/java --threshold-line 0.95 --threshold-branch 0.90 --fail-on-vacuous
changedFilesCoverage.observation: Passes with changed_source_files=0 and vacuous_reason=no_changed_source_files, confirming non-executable module AGENTS.md docs are no longer treated as JaCoCo-covered runtime sources

resolvedIssues.description: The original governance fix still left the architecture document tracked only as lowercase `docs/architecture.md`, which could pass on macOS while failing the canonical Linux-safe docs contract.
resolvedIssues.evidence: The follow-up review confirmed `git ls-tree HEAD -- docs/` now shows a real tracked `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` path, the affected links were updated to the uppercase canonical path, and the docs/governance validators all passed.

appliedUpdates.description: Added an additive `codex-review-policy` command so scrutiny/docs workers can run `bash scripts/enforce_codex_review_policy.sh` from `.factory/services.yaml` instead of invoking it ad hoc.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document a narrow exception for CI/helper-script packets where reproducing the failing script and rerunning the helper is the correct verification instead of a TDD-first Java-test workflow.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `docs-pr-merge-blockers-changed-coverage-fix` review passed a script-only change in `scripts/changed_files_coverage.py`, and the reviewer noted the current backend-worker procedure assumes Maven/Java test creation even when the fix is a small CI-helper change.
